steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: "Having climbed the charts for six weeks," is a participial phrase. The blank serves as the subject of the independent clause that follows. The antecedent is "the song" (singular).

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. Rule: the subject pronoun must match the singular antecedent "the song" in number.
  • Choice A — "those": plural demonstrative pronoun; does not agree with singular "the song." ✗
  • Choice B — "they": third-person plural; does not agree with singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — "each": refers to individual items in a group, not to a single noun; does not serve as a subject pronoun here. ✗
  • Choice D — "it": singular third-person pronoun; correctly agrees with singular "the song." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
